topic.name: Music and Song Analysis
topic.description: This topic encompasses the discussion of various songs, their backgrounds, production details, and cultural impacts.

Document 1: I Love You ( Miss Robot ) is a song written, performed and produced by the Buggles
the song's subject having to do with a robot
Document 2: Manic Monday is a song by the American pop rock band The Bangles
Lyrically, it describes a woman who is waking up to go to work on Monday
Document 3: The Christmas Sessions is the first Christmas album by American Christian rock band MercyMe
the album consists of covers of both modern and traditional Christmas songs
Document 4: Shotgun Willie is a 1973 album by Willie Nelson
the album was recorded in the Atlantic Records studio in New York City
Document 5: Let Yourself Go is a song by American punk rock band Green Day
the song was described to be more punk than that of the band’s previous two singles
Document 6: Tonight: Franz Ferdinand is the third studio album by Scottish indie rock band Franz Ferdinand
It has been described as a concept album loosely based around a night of partying
Document 7: Pittsburgh Town is a folk song written by Woody Guthrie
the song speaks of the labor and environmental problems that the city was facing in 1941
Document 8: Cafe Disco is the twenty-seventh episode of the fifth season of The Office
Several songs are featured in the episode
Document 9: Move Like This is the seventh, and, to date, final studio album by American rock band The Cars
the album reached the top ten of the Billboard 200
Document 10: Do You ... is a song by American R & B recording artist Miguel
Its lyrics pose flirtatious, rhetorical questions and feature both metaphorical and literal references to narcotics

topic.name: Naval Warfare History
topic.description: The documents discuss various battleships and cruisers from different navies, focusing on their designs, roles during major conflicts, and historical significance.

Document 1: Mirabeau was one of the six Danton class semi-dreadnought battleships built for the French Navy.
The ship spent most of World War I blockading the Straits of Otranto.
Document 2: Henri IV was a pre-dreadnought battleship of the French Navy built to test some of the ideas of the prominent naval architect Louis-Émile Bertin.
She was sent to reinforce the Allied naval force in the Dardanelles campaign of 1915.
Document 3: Fusō was a central-battery ironclad built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the 1870s.
She participated in the First Sino-Japanese War of 1894 – 95 where she was damaged during the Battle of the Yalu River.
Document 4: The Königsberg class of light cruisers was a group of four ships commissioned into Germany's Imperial Navy shortly before the end of World War I.
Königsberg and Nürnberg saw action at the Second Battle of Heligoland Bight.
Document 5: HMS Brazen was a B-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy around 1930.
The ship escorted convoys and conducted anti-submarine patrols early in World War II.
Document 6: HMS Havant was an H-class destroyer originally ordered by the Brazilian Navy with the name Javary in the late 1930s.
She was initially assigned to escort duties in the Western Approaches.
Document 7: A Glorious Way to Die: The Kamikaze Mission of the Battleship Yamato, April 1945 is a military history book about the suicide mission of the Japanese battleship Yamato.
The book tells the story from both the Japanese and American points of view.
Document 8: SMS Nürnberg was a Königsberg-class light cruiser built during World War I by Germany for the Imperial Navy.
She participated in Operation Albion in October 1917 against the Russian Navy in the Baltic.
Document 9: The Imperatritsa Ekaterina Velikaya was the second ship of the Imperatritsa Mariya-class dreadnoughts of the Imperial Russian Navy.
She engaged the ex-German battlecruiser Yavuz once, but only inflicted splinter damage.
Document 10: The Raid on Genoa was a minor naval engagement fought in the harbour of the Italian city of Genoa during the first year of the French Revolutionary Wars.
To eliminate the threat these isolated frigates posed, Hood ordered a squadron under Rear-Admiral John Gell to investigate the harbour at Genoa.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure
topic.description: The documents discuss various highways and roadways in the United States and Canada, detailing their routes, historical significance, and connections between communities.

Document 2: Maryland Route 32 ( MD 32 ) is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
The state highway also intersects the four primary highways connecting Baltimore and Washington.
Document 3: Highway 5 is a major highway in the Canadian province of Saskatchewan.
Along the route are several heritage sites, an international bird watching area, saline lakes, as well as regional and provincial parks.
Document 4: Interstate 680 ( I-680 ) is a north – south Interstate Highway in Northern California.
Built in the 1920s and designated in 1955, I-680 begins at a junction with I-280 and US 101.
Document 6: Oregon Route 120 ( OR 120 ) is a 2.71-mile-long ( 4.36 km ) unsigned state highway in the U.S. state of Oregon.
The highway is entirely within the city limits of Portland.
Document 8: State Route 433 ( SR 433 ) is a 0.94-mile ( 1.51 km ) long state highway located entirely in Cowlitz County, Washington, United States.
The highway begins midway across the Columbia River on the National Register of Historic Places listed Lewis and Clark Bridge.

topic.name: Pacific Tropical Cyclones
topic.description: A collection of significant tropical cyclones that formed in the Pacific Ocean, detailing their development, impacts, and meteorological histories.

Document 1: Hurricane Emilia was, at the time, the strongest tropical cyclone on record in the central Pacific Ocean
the first of three Category 5 hurricanes of the 1994 hurricane season
Document 2: Hurricane Darby was the fourth named storm of the 1992 Pacific hurricane season
The origins of Darby were from a tropical wave which moved off the coast of Africa
Document 3: Tropical Storm Bonnie was a weak but persistent tropical cyclone that brought heavy rains to the Southeastern United States
the second storm of the season, Bonnie formed from an area of low pressure northeast of the Bahamas
Document 4: The 2007 Pacific hurricane season was a below-average Pacific hurricane season
featuring one major hurricane
Document 5: Hurricane Joanne was one of four tropical cyclones to bring gale-force winds to the Southwestern United States in the 20th century
Hurricane Joanne peaked as a Category 2 hurricane
Document 6: The effects of Hurricane Isabel in New Jersey in 2003 were overall moderate
Hurricane Isabel formed from a tropical wave on September 6 in the tropical Atlantic Ocean
Document 7: Hurricane Alma saw the latest development of the first storm since 1941
the first named storm of the 1962 Atlantic hurricane season
Document 8: The 1909 Greater Antilles hurricane was a rare, late-season tropical cyclone
caused extensive damage and loss of life in Jamaica and Haiti
Document 9: Tropical Storm Chris caused minor flooding in the Greater Antilles and the Eastern United States
the seventh tropical cyclone and third named storm of the annual hurricane season
Document 10: The 2002 – 03 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was one of the longest lasting and the second-most active season
Storms during the season impacted the Mascarene Islands, Seychelles, Madagascar, and countries in southeastern Africa

topic.name: Historical Sports Figures
topic.description: This topic encompasses notable athletes and sports personalities from various sports and eras, highlighting their achievements and contributions to their respective games.

1: Ernest William Groth was an American Major League Baseball right-handed pitcher who played for three seasons.
He spent the next seven seasons pitching in the minor leagues, retiring at the end of the 1956 season.
2: Joseph A. 'Joe' Small was a West Indian cricketer who played in West Indies' first Test.
He was one of the few black batsmen in the West Indies team at the time.
3: Walter Raleigh Gilbert was an amateur English cricketer who played first-class cricket for Middlesex and Gloucestershire.
He became a professional in 1886, but played only one match before he was caught stealing from teammates.
4: Alan Falconer Kippax was a cricketer for New South Wales and Australia.
He is best remembered for a performance in domestic cricket — a world record last wicket partnership.
5: Henry 'Harry' Fox played cricket and rugby for his county, and began climbing mountains in the mid-1880s.
He played cricket for Somerset County Cricket Club from 1877 to 1882.
6: E. Roy Lester is an American former college and high school football coach.
He was inducted into the West Virginia University Sports Hall of Fame in 2008.
7: Rickey Nelson Henley Henderson is an American retired professional baseball left fielder who played in Major League Baseball for nine teams.
He is widely regarded as baseball's most reliable leadoff hitter and baserunner.
8: Weldy Wilberforce Walker was an American baseball player who became the second African American to play Major League Baseball.
His March 1888 open letter to The Sporting Life protesting the racial segregation of baseball has been described as a passionate cry for justice.
9: The Philadelphia Phillies' 2014 season was the 132nd in the history of the franchise.
They entered the offseason with a strategy to reload rather than rebuild.
10: Ibrahim Khalil 'Abe' Mickal was a Lebanese American college football player and a doctor.
He was a charter member of the LSU Athletic Hall of Fame in 1937.

topic.name: Mushroom Species and Taxonomy
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of mushrooms, their characteristics, taxonomy, and ecological relationships.

Document 1: Lactarius alnicola, commonly known as the golden milkcap, is a species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The species was originally described by American mycologist Alexander H. Smith in 1960.
Document 2: Lactarius rufulus, commonly known as the rufous candy cap, is a species of fungus in the Russulaceae family.
The species was first described by American mycologist Charles Horton Peck in 1907.
Document 3: Amanita exitialis, also known as the Guangzhou destroying angel, is a mushroom of the large genus Amanita.
Molecular analysis shows that the species has a close phylogenetic relationship with three other toxic white Amanitas.
Document 4: Tylopilus tabacinus is a species of bolete fungus in the Boletaceae family.
The species was first described by American mycologist Charles Horton Peck in 1896.
Document 5: Amanita daucipes is a species of fungus in the Amanitaceae family of the Agaricales order of mushrooms.
Amanita daucipes was first described in 1856 by the mycologists Miles Joseph Berkeley and Camille Montagne.
Document 6: Amanita abrupta, commonly known as the American abrupt-bulbed Lepidella, is a species of fungus in the Amanitaceae family of mushrooms.
Amanita abrupta was first described by American mycologist Charles Horton Peck in 1897.
Document 9: Mycena maculata, commonly known as the reddish-spotted Mycena, is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
The species was first described scientifically by the German mycologist Petter Karsten in 1890.

topic.name: Medieval and Early Modern Castles
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history, architecture, and significance of various castles built during the medieval and early modern periods in England and beyond.

Document 1: Skipsea Castle is a Norman motte and bailey castle near the village of Skipsea, East Riding of Yorkshire, England.
Built around 1086 by Drogo de la Beuvrière, it was designed to secure the newly conquered region.
Document 2: Brownsea Castle, originally a Device Fort constructed by Henry VIII between 1545 and 1547 to protect Poole Harbour.
The castle remained in use after the original invasion scare had passed and was occupied by Parliament during the English Civil War.
Document 3: Okehampton Castle is a medieval motte and bailey castle in Devon, England.
It was built between 1068 and 1086 by Baldwin FitzGilbert following a revolt in Devon against Norman rule.
Document 4: Sand Point in Somerset, England, is the peninsula stretching out from Middle Hope, which has a likely motte-and-bailey castle.
Human use of the sites is shown by a bowl barrow and disc barrow from late Neolithic or Bronze Age.
Document 5: Santa Rosa de la Eminencia castle is a colonial castle built in the seventeenth century by the Spanish monarchy in La Asunción, Venezuela.
The structure comprises three defensive fronts, each one with two bastions, two half bastions and three curtains.
Document 6: Dunstanburgh Castle is a 14th-century fortification on the coast of Northumberland in northern England.
The castle was built by Earl Thomas of Lancaster between 1313 and 1322.
Document 7: Sandsfoot Castle, also known historically as Weymouth Castle, is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Weymouth, Dorset.
It formed part of the King’s Device programme to protect against invasion from France and the Holy Roman Empire.
Document 8: Piel Castle, built in the early-14th century by John Cockerham, was intended to oversee the trade through the local harbour.
The castle was built using stones from the local beach, and featured a large keep with surrounding inner and outer baileys.
Document 9: Bowes Castle was a medieval castle in the village of Bowes in County Durham, England.
Built within the perimeter of the former Roman fort of Lavatrae, the early timber castle was replaced by a more substantial stone structure.
Document 10: St Catherine's Castle is a Henrician castle in Cornwall, built by Thomas Treffry between approximately 1538 and 1540.
It was protected by a curtain wall and the surrounding cliffs.

topic.name: Classic Video Games
topic.description: This topic encompasses influential and iconic video games from the 1980s and early 2000s that shaped the gaming industry and culture.

Document 1: Final Fantasy is a fantasy role-playing video game developed and published by Square in 1987.
The game was originally going to be called Fighting Fantasy, but the name was changed to Final Fantasy.
Document 2: Super Mario Bros. is a platform video game developed and published by Nintendo as a pseudo-sequel to the 1983 game, Mario Bros.
The game has sold enormously well, and was the best-selling game of all time for a single platform for approximately three decades.
Document 3: Donkey Kong Jr. Math is a Nintendo edutainment video game where players must solve math problems in order to win.
It was noted as one of the worst launch video games ever made by publications such as Electronic Gaming Monthly.

British and French warships

part of the long and inconclusive Byzantine – Sassanid War

decisive victory for the Persians

raided the Massachusetts communities

victory for the British Army
Document 7: double battle between the Delian League of Athens and her Allies
part of the Wars of the Delian League

topic.name: Military History and Operations
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various military units, their formations, historical significance, and key operations during significant conflicts such as World War I, World War II, and the American Civil War.

1: The 2 / 48th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army which served during the Second World War.
It is considered to be Australia 's most highly decorated unit of the war.
2: Angus McDonald was a prominent Scottish American military officer, frontiersman, sheriff and landowner in Virginia.
McDonald served in the French and Indian War under General John Forbes.
3: General William Howe was a British Army officer who rose to become Commander-in-Chief of British forces during the American War of Independence.
Howe's record in North America was marked by the successful capture of both New York City and Philadelphia.
4: Central Flying School is a Royal Australian Air Force training unit responsible for training flight instructors.
It was the first military aviation unit to be formed in Australia, in 1913.
5: Friedrich Foertsch was a German general serving during World War II.
He held several senior staff positions, including chief of the general staff of the 18. Armee.
6: General Antoine Jules Joseph Huré was a French army officer noted for his service in Morocco.
He was posted back to Morocco in 1916 to become military commander of the Fes region.
7: Marija Bursać was a Bosnian Serb member of the Yugoslav Partisans during World War II.
She became a Partisan in February 1943, joining the newly formed 10th Krajina Brigade.
8: Frederick Birks was a Welsh-born Australian First World War soldier and recipient of the Victoria Cross.
His actions were later recognised with the Victoria Cross.
9: The 11th New York Volunteer Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment of the Union Army in the early years of the American Civil War.
The regiment was among the first to occupy the territory of a Confederate state.
10: 410 Tactical Fighter Operational Training Squadron is a Royal Canadian Air Force aircraft squadron currently located at Canada 's primary training base for the CF-18.
The squadron was formed during the Second World War as an RCAF squadron under the Royal Air Force.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history and notable figures in professional wrestling, highlighting their contributions and careers across different eras and promotions.

Document 1: Trish Stratus is best known for her tenure with WWE and was inducted into the WWE Hall of Fame in 2013.
Stratus retired from professional wrestling at WWE Unforgiven on September 17, 2006 after winning her seventh WWE Women's Championship.
Document 2: Mitsuyo Maeda was fundamental to the development of Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu, including through his teaching of Carlos Gracie.
Maeda was a Japanese judōka and prizefighter who won more than 2,000 professional fights.
Document 3: Cliff Compton is best known for his work in World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE) and Ring of Honor (ROH).
Compton won the OVW Southern Tag Team Championship on three occasions.
Document 4: John McMahon was an American professional wrestler who specialized in collar-and-elbow wrestling and competed from 1861 to 1891.
McMahon served in the Union army during the American Civil War, gaining notoriety as a wrestler.
Document 5: Melina Perez is best known for her time with World Wrestling Entertainment under the ring name Melina.
Perez became the most successful and decorated female contestant in Tough Enough history.
Document 8: Andrés Alejandro Palomeque González is best known for appearing under the stage name Abismo Negro in AAA.
Palomeque worked for the two most prominent professional wrestling promotions in Mexico: CMLL and AAA.
Document 9: WrestleMania IX was the ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by the World Wrestling Federation.
The event featured major storylines including Yokozuna challenging Bret Hart for the WWF World Heavyweight Championship.
Document 10: The Bella Twins were a professional wrestling tag team which performed on WWE and consisted of twin sisters Brie and Nikki Bella.
Both are former WWE Divas Champions, with Nikki's second reign being the longest in the title's history.

The film deals with the negative effects of the dowry system in India.
Document 2: Pathala Bhairavi is a 1951 Indian bilingual fantasy film written by Pingali Nagendrarao
The film centres on the lives of two strangers and incorporates elements of fantasy.

topic.name: Historical Narratives in Media
topic.description: This topic encompasses the portrayal of historical events and figures through interactive media and video games, as well as the documentation of historical military expansions and cultural influences.

Document 1: Time Gal is an interactive movie video game developed and published by Taito
The game is set in a fictional future where time travel is possible.
Document 2: The southward expansion of the Han dynasty comprises a series of military campaigns and expeditions
Chinese culture influenced the newly conquered territories, and merged with native traditions.

topic.name: Amusement Park Attractions
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amusement park attractions, including roller coasters and water parks, detailing their history, design, and significance.

Document 1: The 1999 National League wild-card tie-breaker game was a one-game extension to Major League Baseball's (MLB) 1999 regular season
The Mets won the game 5 – 0, with starting pitcher Al Leiter pitching a two-hit shutout.
Document 2: Montu is an inverted roller coaster at Busch Gardens Tampa Bay in Tampa, Florida.
Built by Bolliger & Mabillard, it is the park's second roller coaster designed by the Swiss company following the success of Kumba.
Document 3: WhiteWater World is a water park situated in the suburb of Coomera on the Gold Coast, Australia.
The ten Australian beach culture themed attractions cost approximately A $ 56 million.
Document 4: Dominator is a steel floorless roller coaster built by Bolliger & Mabillard at Kings Dominion in Doswell, Virginia.
It originally opened at Six Flags Ohio on May 5, 2000 under the name Batman: Knight Flight.
Document 5: Green Lantern Coaster is a steel roller coaster at Warner Bros. Movie World on the Gold Coast, Queensland, Australia.
The ride is themed after DC Comics' Green Lantern and is located within the park's DC Comics superhero hub.
Document 6: Afterburn is a steel inverted roller coaster located at Carowinds amusement park.
The ride previously operated as Top Gun: The Jet Coaster, before it was renamed Afterburn following Cedar Fair's purchase of the park in 2006.
Document 7: Antarctica: Empire of the Penguin is a theme area at SeaWorld Orlando headlined by a first-of-its-kind motion-based, trackless dark ride.
The attraction opened on May 24, 2013 as the largest attraction at any SeaWorld Entertainment theme park.
Document 9: Apollo's Chariot is steel roller coaster at the Busch Gardens Williamsburg theme park in James City County, Virginia.
The ride was the first Hyper Coaster designed by Swiss firm Bolliger & Mabillard.
Document 10: Thunder Run is a wooden roller coaster at the Kentucky Kingdom amusement park in Louisville, Kentucky.
The ride originally operated from August 1990 through to October 2009, when then-operators Six Flags abandoned the park.

topic.name: Medieval English Bishops and Nobility
topic.description: This topic encompasses the roles, influences, and historical contexts of various medieval bishops and noble figures in England and Scotland.

1: Robert of Melun was an English scholastic Christian theologian who taught in France, and later became Bishop of Hereford in England.
Robert was involved in the Council of Reims in 1148, which condemned the teachings of Gilbert de la Porrée.
3: Hugh de Puiset was a medieval Bishop of Durham and Chief Justiciar of England under King Richard I.
Hugh was elected bishop of Durham despite the opposition of Murdac.
4: Edward I was King of England from 1272 to 1307.
Edward spent much of his reign reforming royal administration and common law.
5: Æthelnoth was a medieval Archbishop of Canterbury.
He consecrated a number of other bishops who came from outside his archdiocese.
6: Henry de Lichton was a medieval Scottish prelate and diplomat, who served as Bishop of Moray and Bishop of Aberdeen.
He became a significant patron of the church, a cathedral builder, and a writer.
9: Nicholas de Balmyle was a Scottish administrator and prelate in the late 13th century and early 14th century.
He became Bishop of Dunblane and held this position until his death.

Document 4: The Norwegian Forest cat is a breed of domestic cat originating in Northern Europe.

Scholars have found parallels within the poem to Ovid's Tristia and Amores.
Document 9: The poem is an expansion of the entertainer Charles Dibdin's song of the same name.
A series of comic stanzas on French and English stereotypes.
Document 10: The poem consists of a series of elegies to the men of the Brittonic kingdom of Gododdin.

topic.name: Human Space Exploration
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of human space exploration, including historic missions, spacecraft design, and contributions from notable scientists.

1: Advanced Gemini is a number of proposals that would have extended the Gemini program by the addition of various missions
Gemini was the second manned spaceflight program operated by NASA
2: Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter is a multipurpose spacecraft designed to conduct reconnaissance and exploration of Mars from orbit
It was launched August 12, 2005, and attained Martian orbit on March 10, 2006
3: Soyuz TM-30 was a Soyuz mission, the 39th and final human spaceflight to the Mir space station
The mission was the first privately funded mission to a space station
4: Leo James Rainwater shared the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1975 for his part in determining the asymmetrical shapes of certain atomic nuclei
He worked on the Manhattan Project that developed the first atomic bombs
5: John Tucker 'Chick' Hayward helped develop one of the two atomic bombs that was dropped on Japan
He was a pioneer in the development of nuclear propulsion, nuclear weapons, guidance systems for rockets
6: Luis Walter Alvarez was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1968
He contributed to a number of World War II radar projects
7: Leona Woods was the youngest and only female member of the team which built and experimented with the world 's first nuclear reactor
She worked with Fermi on the Manhattan Project
8: Outer space is the void that exists between celestial bodies, including the Earth
It consists of a hard vacuum containing a low density of particles
9: Marie Curie conducted pioneering research on radioactivity
She was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize

topic.name: Sacred Music and Spiritual Themes
topic.description: The documents explore various forms of sacred music and their connections to spiritual themes in Christianity and Hinduism.

1: Varaha Upanishad is a minor Upanishad of Hinduism
emphasizes that liberation from sorrow and fear requires a human being to know the non-dualistic nature of existence
2: Locus iste is a sacred motet composed by Anton Bruckner
intended for the dedication of the Votivkapelle at the New Cathedral in Linz, Austria
3: The Magnificat by John Rutter is a musical setting of the biblical canticle Magnificat
based on the Latin text, interspersed with Of a Rose, a lovely Rose
4: Orfeo ed Euridice is an opera based on the myth of Orpheus
attempted to replace the abstruse plots and overly complex music of opera seria
5: Sie werden aus Saba alle kommen is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach
composed it in 1724 in Leipzig for Epiphany
6: St John Passion is a sacred oratorio by Johann Sebastian Bach
written to be performed in a Lutheran service on Good Friday
7: Erforsche mich, Gott, und erfahre mein Herz is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach
part of Bach's first annual cycle of cantatas
8: Ihr werdet weinen und heulen is a cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach
based on the Gospel reading from the Farewell Discourse
9: Jessie Bond was an English singer and actress known for creating mezzo-soprano soubrette roles in Gilbert and Sullivan comic operas
spent twenty years on the stage
10: Man singet mit Freuden vom Sieg is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach
composed the work in Leipzig for the Feast of Saint Michael

topic.name: Islamic Military Campaigns
topic.description: The documents discuss various military campaigns and sieges during the early expansion of the Islamic Caliphate, focusing on key battles and their historical significance.

Document 1: The Siege of Kamarja was fought in 729 between the Turgesh khaganate and its Soghdian allies and the Arab Muslims of the Umayyad Caliphate.
Its stubborn defence, which lasted for 58 days, ended with the negotiated withdrawal of its garrison to Samarkand.
Document 2: Qutayba ibn Muslim was an Arab commander of the Umayyad Caliphate army who became governor of Khurasan and distinguished himself in the conquest of Transoxiana.
He consolidated Muslim rule in the area and expanded the Caliphate's border to include most of Transoxiana.
Document 5: The Battle of the Trench was a 27-day long siege of Yathrib (now Medina) by Arab and Jewish tribes.
The largely outnumbered defenders of Medina, mainly Muslims led by Islamic prophet Muhammad, dug a trench on the suggestion of Salman Farsi.

topic.name: Formula One Racing
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of Formula One racing events, including specific races, car developments, and notable drivers.

Document 1: The McLaren M2B was the McLaren team's first Formula One racing car, used during the 1966 season.
Driven by team founder Bruce McLaren, the M2B had a short Grand Prix career.
Document 2: The 2015 Chinese Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race that was held on 12 April 2015.
Hamilton took pole position during Saturday's qualifying, the 41st of his career and the third in a row.
Document 4: The 2000 German Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 30 July 2000 at the Hockenheimring.
The race was won by Ferrari driver Rubens Barrichello after starting from 18th position.
Document 7: The 2015 Austrian Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 21 June 2015 at the Red Bull Ring.
Hamilton started from pole position, but was overtaken by Rosberg at the first corner.

topic.name: Science Fiction Magazines and Media
topic.description: This topic encompasses the history and impact of various science fiction magazines and television series, highlighting their contributions to the genre and cultural significance.

Document 1: Wonder Story Annual was a science fiction pulp magazine which was launched in 1950 by Standard Magazines.
It lasted for four issues, succumbing in 1953 to competition from the growing market for paperback science fiction.
Document 2: Flash Gordon is a science fiction television series based on the characters of the Alex Raymond-created comic strip of the same name.
The series proved popular with American audiences and critical response, though sparse, was positive.
Document 4: Miracle Science and Fantasy Stories was a pulp science fiction magazine which published two issues in 1931.
The magazine ceased publication when Dold became ill and was unable to continue his duties both as editor and artist.
Document 6: Out of This World Adventures was a pulp magazine which published two issues, in July and December 1950.
Sales were weak, and after two issues Avon decided to cancel it.
Document 8: Vargo Statten Science Fiction Magazine published nineteen issues between 1954 and 1956.
The editor was initially Alistair Paterson, but after seven issues Fearn took the helm.

topic.name: Mathematics and Its Applications
topic.description: The documents discuss various mathematical concepts and their applications in different fields.

Document 1: Taylor series is a representation of a function as an infinite sum of terms.
A function can be approximated by using a finite number of terms of its Taylor series.
Document 2: The binary logarithm is the power to which the number 2 must be raised to obtain the value n.
Binary logarithms can be used to calculate the length of the representation of a number in the binary numeral system.
Document 4: A pseudoforest is an undirected graph in which every connected component has at most one cycle.
Pseudoforests also form graph-theoretic models of functions.
Document 6: A matrix is a rectangular array of numbers, symbols, or expressions.
A major application of matrices is to represent linear transformations.
Document 8: The curve-shortening flow modifies a smooth curve in the Euclidean plane.
The circle is the only simple closed curve that maintains its shape under the curve-shortening flow.
Document 10: Design management is a business discipline that uses project management, design, strategy, and supply chain techniques to control a creative process.
Design management encompasses the ongoing processes, business decisions, and strategies that enable innovation.

topic.name: U.S. Constitutional Law
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. constitutional law, including amendments, legal cases, and state constitutions.

1: The Twenty-fifth Amendment to the United States Constitution deals with succession to the Presidency
It supersedes the ambiguous wording of Article II, Section 1, Clause 6 of the Constitution
3: United States v. Alvarez, 567 U.S. _ _ _ (2012), was a United States Supreme Court case
The Court struck down the Stolen Valor Act, a federal law that criminalized false statements about having a military medal
4: A Non-constituency Member of Parliament (NCMP) is a member of a Singaporean opposition political party
The NCMP scheme was introduced in 1984, and was a significant modification of the traditional Westminster system of government
6: The Constitution of the State of New Jersey is the basic governing document of the State of New Jersey
The current document was issued in 1947 and has been amended several times
7: The Fourth Amendment prohibits unreasonable searches and seizures
It is part of the Bill of Rights and was adopted in response to the abuse of the writ of assistance

topic.name: Financial and Business History
topic.description: The documents collectively explore significant figures and institutions in the realms of finance, commerce, and entrepreneurship across different historical contexts.

Document 4: Chemical Bank was a leading bank in the U.S. that played a major role in the consolidation of the banking industry through numerous acquisitions.
Document 5: Wellington R. Burt was a wealthy lumber baron known for his unusual will and significant wealth, ranking among the wealthiest men in America during his time.

topic.name: Philosophical Concepts and Theories
topic.description: This cluster discusses various philosophical theories and concepts, including theodicy, ethics, and the philosophy of science.

1: Irenaean theodicy is a Christian theodicy designed to respond to the problem of evil.
It defends the probability of an omnipotent and omnibenevolent God in the face of evidence of evil in the world.
4: Kantian ethics refers to a deontological ethical theory ascribed to the German philosopher Immanuel Kant.
Central to Kant's construction of the moral law is the categorical imperative, which acts on all people, regardless of their interests or desires.
9: Philosophy of science is a branch of philosophy concerned with the foundations, methods, and implications of science.
The central questions of this study concern what qualifies as science, the reliability of scientific theories, and the ultimate purpose of science.

topic.name: Historical Development in Texas
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of the historical development and demographic changes in Texas, focusing on notable figures, communities, and events that shaped the region.

1: Louis F. Menage was a real estate speculator and prominent figure in early Minneapolis, Minnesota history.
Menage earned a fortune developing land on the city's borders into residential housing.
3: Gulfton experienced rapid development, with new apartment complexes built for young individuals from the Northeast and Midwest United States.
Gulfton became a predominantly immigrant community.
4: Andrew Davis Bruce served as the third president of the University of Houston.
Bruce was born in St. Louis, Missouri and moved to Texas as a child.
5: Bellaire is a city in southwest Harris County, Texas, within the Houston – Sugar Land – Baytown metropolitan area.
Bellaire was founded in 1908 by William Wright Baldwin.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Institutions
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable historical figures and institutions related to politics, religion, and community development in the United States and the UK.

1: George Nixon Briggs was an American lawyer and politician from Massachusetts.
Briggs served for twelve years in the United States House of Representatives.
2: St Ffinan's Church, Llanffinan is a small 19th-century parish church built in the Romanesque revival style.
The church is still used for worship by the Church in Wales.
3: Thomas Quiney was the husband of William Shakespeare's daughter Judith Shakespeare.
Quiney held several municipal offices in the corporation of Stratford-upon-Avon.
4: Vicky Gene Robinson is an American retired bishop of the Diocese of New Hampshire in the Episcopal Church.
Robinson is widely known for being the first priest in an openly gay relationship to be consecrated a bishop.
5: Citibase Brighton is a complex of serviced offices on a prominent elevated position in the Round Hill area of Brighton.
The large Gothic Revival building has had three greatly different uses since its construction.
6: Congregation Beth Jacob Ohev Sholom is the oldest Orthodox congregation on Long Island.
The congregation was formed in 1869 by German Jews as an Orthodox breakaway.
7: Jessica Feshbach was an American official within the Scientology organization.
Feshbach is most well known for her stint as Tom Cruise's assistant.
8: Agudath Israel Etz Ahayem is a Conservative Jewish congregation located in Montgomery, Alabama.
Agudath Israel was established as an Orthodox synagogue in 1902.
9: Anshei Israel Synagogue is a historic synagogue located in Lisbon, Connecticut.
The Orthodox congregation was founded with 15 families and constructed the synagogue in 1936.
10: Evan Bayh began a movement for the 2008 Democratic nomination for president of the United States.
Bayh stopped short of a full-fledged candidacy, withdrawing from the race in December 2006.

4: Broder Lysholm Knudtzon was a Norwegian merchant, politician and benefactor.

topic.name: Historical Sites and Developments

4: British General Charles Cornwallis was appointed in February 1786 to serve as both Commander-in-Chief of British India

3: Larry David is an American comedian, writer, actor, and television producer known for creating Seinfeld and Curb Your Enthusiasm.

topic.name: World War II Military Leaders

Document 2: Major General William Curtis Chase is best known for his service in the South West Pacific Area during World War II.
